definitions.definition: Principles of Management are general truths, universally applicable, which are derived from the experiences of successful managers and can be used as guidelines by all managers. These are also known as maxims.
definitions.example: An example of a principle is 'Unity of direction' where all the members of an organization are working towards the same goal.

definitions.definition: Unity of direction implies that all the members of an organization are working towards the same goal. It ensures that everyone in the organization is working in the same direction.
definitions.example: A company's objective is to increase its market share, so all the employees work towards this goal.

definitions.definition: Unity of command implies that each employee reports to only one superior or manager. This ensures that there is no confusion regarding who to report to.
definitions.example: A manager is responsible for a team of employees, and they all report directly to the manager.

definitions.definition: Division of labour implies breaking down of tasks into smaller and more manageable sub-tasks. This increases efficiency and productivity.
definitions.example: A production manager breaks down the task of manufacturing a car into several sub-tasks such as engine production, body production, etc.

definitions.definition: Unity of effort implies that all the departments and units of an organization work together to achieve the common goal. This ensures that everyone is working together towards the same objective.
definitions.example: All the departments of a company work together to achieve the company's objective of increasing its market share.

definitions.definition: Unity of action implies that all the departments and units of an organization take action simultaneously to achieve the common goal. This ensures that everyone is working together towards the same objective.
definitions.example: All the departments of a company take action simultaneously to achieve the company's objective of increasing its market share.

definitions.definition: Line and staff organisation implies a type of organisation where line employees are responsible for production and staff employees provide support services.
definitions.example: A production manager is a line employee, while a human resource manager is a staff employee.

definitions.definition: Line organisation implies a type of organisation where employees are responsible for production and are directly involved in achieving the organisation's goals.
definitions.example: A production manager is a line employee.

definitions.definition: Staff organisation implies a type of organisation where employees provide support services and are not directly involved in achieving the organisation's goals.
definitions.example: A human resource manager is a staff employee.

definitions.definition: Functional organisation implies a type of organisation where departments are grouped based on their function or area of expertise.
definitions.example: A company has a marketing department, a sales department, and a production department.

definitions.definition: Departmentalisation implies breaking down of an organisation into smaller departments or units to increase efficiency and productivity.
definitions.example: A company has a production department, a marketing department, and a sales department.

definitions.definition: Authority implies the right to give orders, make decisions and take actions within an organisation.
definitions.example: A manager has the authority to make decisions regarding the production process.

definitions.definition: Responsibility implies the obligation to perform tasks and achieve goals within an organisation.
definitions.example: A manager is responsible for the production process.

definitions.definition: Accountability implies being answerable for one's actions and decisions within an organisation.
definitions.example: A manager is accountable for the production process.

definitions.definition: Centralisation implies the concentration of power and decision-making authority in the hands of top management.
definitions.example: A company has a top-down decision-making structure.

definitions.definition: Decentralisation implies the distribution of power and decision-making authority among various levels of management.
definitions.example: A company has a flat organisational structure.
